body.intention: I intend that no ticket becomes claimable without an intention naming a property a hollow build would violate — and that a DEGENERATE intention ('unknown', 'TBD', '?') is refused exactly as a missing one is, because a bare presence-check accepts the very string a crashing extractor writes.

body.narrative: RED at 8f76f01d (stub-first): gate wired at all six sprint write-paths but accepts the literal string 'unknown' — precisely what the ~99%-broken intent extractor writes into 2,435 records. GREEN at b73c14b4: 'unknown' is REFUSED. THE HOLLOW BUILD THIS DEFEATS: 'if not t[intention]: reject' passes a green run and certifies every one of those crash outputs as a valid intention. Also proven: enforced at ALL SIX sprint write-paths (setstatus refuses loudly, exit 1; reset/ungate/stale-reconcile DIVERT to triage, so a ticket is never STRANDED by a gate that only meant to hold a line), and the causes stay DISTINCT (missing/empty/degenerate are three reasons, not one).
body.why: The intention IS the property mutation-red breaks. A ticket with none has no property to break, so it CANNOT be proven — it can only close hollow. Measured 2026-07-13: 149 sprint tickets carried no intention. ONE absent gate, not 149 oversights.
